codeReview.summary: Comprehensive implementation of cross-schema consistency tests (30 tests across 5 classes) that thoroughly validate VAL-CROSS-02 and VAL-CROSS-03. The tests use AST-based static analysis to detect schema redefinitions, runtime identity checks to confirm modules share the same class objects, Pydantic validation enforcement tests, config resolution tests across all subsystems, and round-trip serialization tests. All 30 tests pass, type-checker passes, and the approach is sound.

issues.description: The test_all_downstream_modules_import_from_schemas method checks source code string patterns ('from grist_mill.schemas' in source and schema_name in source) rather than verifying actual import resolution. While pragmatic, this could theoretically produce false negatives if a schema name appears in a comment or docstring. Consider supplementing with hasattr-based checks on the module objects for extra robustness. Not blocking — the current approach is effective and all tests pass.

The AST-based scan in test_no_class_definitions_outside_schemas only checks top-level class definitions (ast.iter_child_nodes) rather than nested classes. This is acceptable since schema classes wouldn't be nested, but worth noting.

summary: The feature m1-cross-schema-consistency is well-implemented and passes review. 30 tests in 5 test classes cover: (1) AST-based verification that no core schema classes are redefined outside grist_mill.schemas/, (2) AST-based check that enums aren't reassigned, (3) verification that 5 key downstream modules import from grist_mill.schemas, (4) runtime identity checks confirming Task, TaskResult, TaskStatus, ErrorCategory, HarnessConfig, and TelemetrySchema are the same class objects across modules, (5) Pydantic validation enforcement for required fields, score bounds, and status enums, (6) config resolution across agent/environment/telemetry subsystems from a single YAML, (7) env var and CLI override propagation, (8) JSON round-trip serialization for all schema types, and (9) telemetry forward compatibility. All tests pass, ty check passes, and the implementation aligns with the feature's expected behavior. VAL-CROSS-02 (config resolution across subsystems) and VAL-CROSS-03 (schema consistency from definition to consumption) are both satisfied.
